description_long: This is a solid, metallic open-ended wrench with a robust, utilitarian design. The tool features a thick, straight handle that tapers slightly toward the open jaw, which is angled and has flat gripping surfaces for turning nuts or bolts. The surface appears matte and dark, likely made from forged steel, with no visible branding or decorative elements. The handle is smooth, lacking any knurling or rubberized grip, and the entire object is a single, continuous piece of metal. The jaws are fixed and parallel, suitable for standard hexagonal fasteners. There are no signs of rust, wear, or additional attachments, giving it a clean, well-maintained appearance.
description: A sturdy, matte-finished open-ended wrench with a smooth handle and fixed jaws, made from dark metal. The tool is simple, robust, and free of visible branding or wear.
description_view_0: Side profile showing the full length of the wrench, highlighting the thickness of the handle and the angled open jaw.
description_view_1: Front view of the open jaw, clearly displaying the parallel, flat gripping surfaces and the width of the opening.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, again showing the handle and jaw, with the jaw's thickness and angle visible.
description_view_3: Rear view of the handle, showing its width and the solid, unadorned back end.
